method_atom: A parametric statistical-inference procedure that jointly fits a small set of parameters of a generative model to several independent measurement datasets, using likelihood/confidence-interval estimation to constrain each parameter, to exclude alternative hypotheses at stated confidence levels, and to detect the imprint of specific underlying processes as features in spectral (power-spectrum) and spatial-correlation observables. It combines heterogeneous data channels to break degeneracies and cross-validates a predicted effect against independent measurements of the same quantity.
object_atom: The standard cosmological model (LCDM) and its extensions describing the universe: the cosmic microwave background temperature and polarization anisotropies, the primordial power spectrum, neutrino masses and effective species count, primordial helium from big bang nucleosynthesis, and the Sunyaev-Zel'dovich signal in clusters of galaxies.
brief_reason: The multi-dataset parametric model-fitting, hypothesis-exclusion, and spectral-feature-detection machinery is transferable across fields, while the CMB/LCDM universe and its cosmological constituents are the physics-specific object.
